senses.definition: to inspect something closely to ensure accuracy, quality, or its overall condition

examples.x: She spent hours checking over the accounts for any discrepancies.
The teacher checked over the students' homework assignments.
I need to check over the equipment before the event.
The supervisor checked over the completed project thoroughly.

senses.definition: to briefly explain or describe something, often leaving out complex or technical details

examples.x: In the meeting, he quickly glossed over the financial report, not delving into the specifics of the budget.
When writing the summary, she chose to gloss over the lengthy legal details, focusing on the main conclusions.
The professor didn't want to gloss over the challenging topics but ensured students had resources for in-depth understanding.
During the training session, the instructor tended to gloss over the intricacies of the software, leaving some participants confused.

senses.definition: to thoroughly review, examine, or check something

examples.x: Please go over your essay for any grammatical errors before submitting it.
The accountant will go over the financial statements to ensure accuracy.
It's important to go over the terms and conditions before signing the contract.
The teacher asked the students to go over their homework assignments.
We need to go over the details of the project to make sure nothing is missed.

senses.definition: to examine or inspect something quickly

examples.x: I'm going to look over your proposal before submitting it.
The teacher briefly looked over the students' essays before providing feedback.
The mechanic is looking over the car to see what's wrong with it.
I looked over the contract carefully before signing it.
They will look over the financial reports before making any investment decisions.

senses.definition: to think carefully about something for a long time

examples.x: I need to mull over my options before I make a decision.
She mulled over the proposal for several days before she gave her answer.
The politician spent weeks mulling over his resignation.
The group mulled over the problem for hours before they came up with a solution.

senses.definition: to hide problems, disagreements, or differences instead of addressing them fully or resolving them

examples.x: It's not wise to paper over such a serious problem; it needs to be addressed directly.
The government can't just paper over these social issues; they need genuine solutions.
The company decided to paper over the financial discrepancies instead of investigating them.
They tried to paper over their differences, but deep down, the conflict remained.

senses.definition: to skip or ignore something or someone

examples.x: Let's pass over the details for now and focus on the bigger picture.
She felt like her contributions were consistently passed over during meetings.
It's important not to pass over any safety procedures.
They often pass over the details when reviewing the contract.
They passed over his mistakes.

senses.definition: to examine something closely and attentively

examples.x: She pored over the old letters, searching for clues about her ancestry.
Before signing the contract, it's essential to pore over its terms and conditions.
The detective pored over the evidence, looking for any inconsistencies.
The lawyers pored over the legal documents for hours, ensuring there were no loopholes.
As the exam neared, he pored over his notes every night.

senses.definition: to review something from start to finish in order to identify errors or gain a better understanding

examples.x: He decided to read over the contract carefully to ensure he understood all the terms.
As a proofreading step, she likes to read over her essays one more time before handing them in.

senses.definition: to review a text or information

examples.x: The researcher had to run over the data to verify the accuracy of the findings.
The editor will run over the manuscript to catch any last-minute errors.
The manager had to run over the financial report to ensure its accuracy before the meeting.
The student ran over the answers in the textbook to prepare for the quiz.
The coach asked the team to run over the game plan one more time before the match.

senses.definition: to consider a matter carefully before reaching a decision

examples.x: She needs time to think over whether to accept the invitation.
Before buying a house, it's wise to think over your financial situation.
We should think over the consequences of our actions.

senses.definition: to be in charge of someone or something and to protect them from any harm

examples.x: As a supervisor, it's your job to watch over the team's progress.
The shepherd faithfully watches over the flock in the pasture.
The bodyguard watches over the celebrity discreetly in public.
